title: Percentage Calculator

hero.title: Percentage Calculator
hero.subtitle: Discover exact percentage values, calculate exact retail discounts, or measure historical growth deltas instantly.

options.label: I want to apply a Discount (or Increase) of X% to a price
I want to find out the % change between two values
I want to know what percentage a smaller part represents of the total

formula_explanation: Percentages mathematically represent parts per hundred. By utilizing straightforward ratios, you can calculate portions, total changes, and comparative discounts.
example: If a $150 item is 20% off, calculating '20% of 150' gives $30. The brand new final amount is $120. If $20 grows to $150, that's a +650% exponential percentage change.

faq.question: How do I calculate what percentage a number is of another?
faq.answer: Simple division. Divide the specific part by the whole amount, then mathematically multiply by 100.

faq.question: What does a negative percentage change mean?
faq.answer: A negative change literally means the new value is less than the original value, representing a mathematical decrease, deficit, or discount.

faq.question: How do I apply a retail discount?
faq.answer: Calculate X% (the discount) of Y (the original retail price). Then subtract that result from Y to find your final sale price.
